{"step_by_step_thinking": "Saponins are known to have immunomodulatory properties. In the document titled 'Respiratory Syncytial Virus Prefusion F (RSVPreF3) Vaccine -- Mechanism of Action', it is explicitly stated that the AS01 adjuvant in the RSVPreF3 vaccine contains QS-21, which is extracted from the bark of the Quillaja saponaria tree. QS-21 is a saponin and is described as one of the two immunostimulants in the AS01 adjuvant. The AS01 adjuvant enhances immune responses by activating innate immune cells, inducing cytokine production, promoting antigen presentation, and stimulating both humoral and cellular immunity. This clearly demonstrates that saponins, specifically QS-21, are used as adjuvants in vaccines. Therefore, based on the provided evidence, saponins can be used as adjuvants.", "answer_choice": "A"}
